Author: essen__
Content: Nice, how did you guys go about changing the default cell library ?

Author: rzioma
Content:
@Essen theoretically it should be just `"STD_CELL_LIBRARY": "gf180mcu_fd_sc_mcu9t5v0",` in `config.json`
BUT there is a bug in the PDK that TT is using, so @htamas had to make a patch: https://github.com/TinyTapeout/tt-gds-action/compare/main...rejunity:tt-gds-action-gf180mcu_fd_sc_mcu9t5v0:main

Author: rzioma
Content:
So basically you have to
1) clone https://github.com/TinyTapeout/tt-gds-action apply the patch
1.5) add a tag on your patched gds-action repo, I used `ttgf0p2_9t`
2) route your gds.yaml to use patched gds-action repo. something like that: https://github.com/rejunity/tt08-vga-drop/commit/2ac440a4bd84136deccb49f6ed56c4ded1a0a8b7
3) add "STD_CELL_LIBRARY": "gf180mcu_fd_sc_mcu9t5v0", to your `config.json`
4) change Makefile so that Gate Level tests use correct cell library too: https://github.com/rejunity/tt08-vga-drop/commit/22135b09cfd3b970ce26098b89df31547c557192

and you should be done

Author: rzioma
Content: You might get around 10..15% speedup from that

Author: 246tnt
Content: Well the 2000$ option is only as an addition to the 7000$ slot.

Author: 246tnt
Content: So it's just to get a neat souvenir ... or if you want to do wafer level probing I guess.

Author: essen__
Content: So sad, you got my hopes up there for a second. 🤣

Author: 246tnt
Content: There is a fixed number of slots in the reticle ...  so the price is pretty much production_cost / num_slots ...  2k$ is the cost to run one more wafer through the flow.

Author: 246tnt
Content: The production costs are (simplified)  maskset_cost  + n_wafer * wafer_processing_cost    and the mask costs is the big chunks and the part which has a limited number of slots.
